text.title: A/B Test and SaaS Lifecycle Tracking Patterns
text.summary: Patterns for tracking experiment exposure, SaaS user lifecycle events, feature usage, content engagement, and video.

section.title: A/B Test Tracking

rule.statement: Emit an `experiment_exposure` event at the moment the variant is rendered, and set it as a user property; without exposure tracking, post-hoc segment analysis is unreliable.
rule.rationale: If exposure is not recorded, any user who saw a variant but did not convert is invisible to the analysis. Survivor bias inflates measured lift.

section.title: SaaS User Lifecycle Events

rule.statement: Track the five lifecycle events — signup, activation, return visit, purchase, referral — with consistent naming across web, mobile, and backend; use server-side Stripe webhook as the authoritative purchase source.
rule.rationale: Client-side purchase events are lost to ad blockers, ITP, and tab-close race conditions. Server-side purchase events from Stripe webhooks are the only complete record and should override client-side fallbacks.

section.title: Feature Usage and Content Engagement

rule.statement: Track feature usage with a generic `feature_usage` event carrying `feature_name` and `feature_action`; track article read progress at 25/50/75/100% milestones and video progress at 25/50/75% milestones.
rule.rationale: Milestone-based progress tracking avoids event flooding while preserving the shape of engagement curves needed for content optimization and onboarding analysis.
